6.0.description: Sample liner responses. The nodes of an element will be ordered counterclockwise with respect to the outside of the zone faces upon which the liner was created.

keywords.name: coupling-displacement-normal
keywords.description: displacement in the normal coupling spring at a node of the element. Sign convention for a normal spring is +/-, indicating separation/overlap. This option uses the node and side keywords.

keywords.name: coupling-displacement-shear
keywords.description: displacement in the shear coupling spring at a node of the element. Shear springs store displacement magnitude, and this is always positive. This option uses the node and side keywords.

keywords.name: coupling-stress-normal
keywords.description: stress in the normal coupling spring at a node of the element. The sign convention for normal spring is +/-, indicating separation/overlap. This option uses the node and side keywords.

keywords.name: coupling-stress-shear
keywords.description: stress in the shear coupling spring at a node of the element. This option uses the node and side keywords.

keywords.name: coupling-yield
keywords.description: yield state in the coupling spring at a node of the element. Value {0, 1, 2} denotes never yielded, now yielding, or yielded in the past, respectively. This option uses the node and side keywords.

keywords.name: force
keywords.description: force magnitude, global system. This option uses the node keyword.

keywords.name: force-x
keywords.description: nodal force (x-component, global system). This option uses the node keyword.

keywords.name: force-y
keywords.description: nodal force (y-component, global system). This option uses the node keyword.

keywords.name: force-z
keywords.description: nodal force (z-component, global system). This option uses the node keyword.

keywords.name: moment
keywords.description: moment magnitude, global system. This option uses the node keyword.

keywords.name: moment-x
keywords.description: nodal moment (x-component, global system). This option uses the node keyword.

keywords.name: moment-y
keywords.description: nodal moment (y-component, global system). This option uses the node keyword.

keywords.name: moment-z
keywords.description: nodal moment (z-component, global system). This option uses the node keyword.

keywords.name: resultant-mx
keywords.description: stress resultant Mx in the element surface system. This option uses the surface-x keyword.

keywords.name: resultant-my
keywords.description: stress resultant My in the element surface system. This option uses the surface-x keyword.

keywords.name: resultant-mxy
keywords.description: stress resultant Mxy in the element surface system. This option uses the surface-x keyword.

keywords.name: resultant-nx
keywords.description: stress resultant Nx in the element surface system. This option uses the surface-x keyword.

keywords.name: resultant-ny
keywords.description: stress resultant Ny in the element surface system. This option uses the surface-x keyword.

keywords.name: resultant-nxy
keywords.description: stress resultant Nxy in the element surface system. This option uses the surface-x keyword.

keywords.name: resultant-qx
keywords.description: stress resultant Qx in the element surface system. This option uses the surface-x keyword.

keywords.name: resultant-qy
keywords.description: stress resultant Qy in the element surface system. This option uses the surface-x keyword.

keywords.name: principal-intermediate
keywords.description: principal intermediate stress, global system. This option uses the depth-factor keyword.

keywords.name: principal-maximum
keywords.description: principal maximum stress, global system. This option uses the depth-factor keyword.

keywords.name: principal-minimum
keywords.description: principal minimum stress, global system. This option uses the depth-factor keyword.

keywords.name: stress-xx
keywords.description: stress xx-component, global system. This option uses the depth-factor keyword.

keywords.name: stress-xy
keywords.description: stress xy-component, global system. This option uses the depth-factor keyword.

keywords.name: stress-xz
keywords.description: stress xz-component, global system. This option uses the depth-factor keyword.

keywords.name: stress-yy
keywords.description: stress yy-component, global system. This option uses the depth-factor keyword.

keywords.name: stress-yz
keywords.description: stress yz-component, global system. This option uses the depth-factor keyword.

keywords.name: stress-zz
keywords.description: stress zz-component, global system. This option uses the depth-factor keyword.

keywords.name: component-id
keywords.description: the element is selected by its individual component ID number

keywords.name: depth-factor
keywords.description: the depth factor used to determine element stresses. Only available if noted.

keywords.name: node
keywords.description: the node location to use when retrieving values from the element. Must be from 1 to 3. Only available if noted.

keywords.name: position
keywords.description: the element with a centroid closest to v is selected

keywords.name: side
keywords.description: specifies the side of an embedded liner from which to retrieve coupling values. By default, this is side 1.

keywords.name: surface-x
keywords.description: the local x-direction used to determine the local surface coordinate system. Only available if noted.

7.0.description: Sample liner responses. The nodes of an element will be ordered counterclockwise with respect to the outside of the zone faces upon which the liner was created.

keywords.name: coupling-displacement-normal
keywords.description: displacement in the normal coupling spring at a node of the element. Sign convention for a normal spring is +/-, indicating separation/overlap. This option uses the node and side keywords.

keywords.name: coupling-displacement-shear
keywords.description: displacement in the shear coupling spring at a node of the element. Shear springs store displacement magnitude, and this is always positive. This option uses the node and side keywords.

keywords.name: coupling-stress-normal
keywords.description: stress in the normal coupling spring at a node of the element. The sign convention for normal spring is +/-, indicating separation/overlap. This option uses the node and side keywords.

keywords.name: coupling-stress-shear
keywords.description: stress in the shear coupling spring at a node of the element. This option uses the node and side keywords.

keywords.name: coupling-yield
keywords.description: yield state in the coupling spring at a node of the element. Value {0, 1, 2} denotes never yielded, now yielding, or yielded in the past, respectively. This option uses the node and side keywords.

keywords.description: force magnitude, global system. This option uses the node keyword.

keywords.name: stress-xy
keywords.description: stress \(xy\)-component, global system. This option uses the depth-factor keyword.

keywords.description: stress \(xz\)-component, global system. This option uses the depth-factor keyword.

keywords.name: component-id
keywords.description: the element is selected by its individual component ID number

keywords.name: depth-factor
keywords.description: the depth factor used to determine element stresses. Only available if noted.

keywords.name: node
keywords.description: the node location to use when retrieving values from the element. Must be from 1 to 3. Only available if noted.

keywords.name: position
keywords.description: the element with a centroid closest to v is selected

keywords.name: side
keywords.description: specifies the side of an embedded liner from which to retrieve coupling values. By default, this is side 1.

keywords.name: surface-x
keywords.description: the local \(x\)-direction used to determine the local surface coordinate system. Only available if noted.

keywords.name: coupling-displacement-normal
keywords.description: displacement in the normal coupling spring at a node of the element. Normal displacement positive/negative implies separation/overlap only if the liner is on the outer surface and liner element normals point away from the body to which the liner is attached. This option uses the node and side keywords.

keywords.name: coupling-displacement-shear
keywords.description: displacement in the shear coupling spring at a node of the element Shear displacement is always positive. This option uses the node and side keywords.

keywords.name: coupling-stress-normal
keywords.description: stress in the normal coupling spring at a node of the element. Normal stress positive/negative implies tension/compression only if the liner is on the outer surface and liner element normals point away from the body to which the liner is attached. This option uses the node and side keywords.

keywords.name: coupling-stress-shear
keywords.description: stress in the shear coupling spring at a node of the element. Shear stress is always positive. This option uses the node and side keywords.

keywords.name: coupling-yield
keywords.description: yield state in the shear coupling spring at a node of the element. Value {0, 1, 2} denotes never yielded, now yielding, or yielded in the past, respectively. This option uses the node and side keywords.

keywords.name: force
keywords.description: nodal force magnitude. Uses the node keyword.

keywords.name: force-x
keywords.description: nodal force ( \(x\) -component, global system). Uses the node keyword.

keywords.name: force-y
keywords.description: nodal force ( \(y\) -component, global system). Uses the node keyword.

keywords.name: force-z
keywords.description: nodal force ( \(z\) -component, global system). Uses the node keyword.

keywords.name: ipstress-xx
keywords.description: stress ( \(xx\) -component, surface coordinate system) for an element with a plastic material model. Uses the integration-point keyword and may also use the surface-x keyword.

keywords.name: ipstress-yy
keywords.description: stress ( \(yy\) -component, surface coordinate system) for an element with a plastic material model. Uses the integration-point keyword and may also use the surface-x keyword.

keywords.name: ipstress-xy
keywords.description: stress ( \(xy\) -component, surface coordinate system) for an element with a plastic material model. Uses the integration-point keyword and may also use the surface-x keyword.

keywords.name: moment
keywords.description: nodal moment magnitude. Uses the node keyword.

keywords.name: moment-x
keywords.description: nodal moment ( \(x\) -component, global system). Uses the node keyword.

keywords.name: moment-y
keywords.description: nodal moment ( \(y\) -component, global system). Uses the node keyword.

keywords.name: moment-z
keywords.description: nodal moment ( \(z\) -component, global system). Uses the node keyword.

keywords.name: resultant-mx
keywords.description: stress resultant \(M_x\) (surface system) at element centroid. May use the surface-x keyword.

keywords.name: resultant-my
keywords.description: stress resultant \(M_y\) (surface system) at element centroid. May use the surface-x keyword.

keywords.name: resultant-mxy
keywords.description: stress resultant \(M_{xy}\) (surface system) at element centroid. May use the surface-x keyword.

keywords.name: resultant-nx
keywords.description: stress resultant \(N_x\) (surface system) at element centroid. May use the surface-x keyword.

keywords.name: resultant-ny
keywords.description: stress resultant \(N_y\) (surface system) at element centroid. May use the surface-x keyword.

keywords.name: resultant-nxy
keywords.description: stress resultant \(N_{xy}\) (surface system) at element centroid. May use the surface-x keyword.

keywords.name: resultant-qx
keywords.description: stress resultant \(Q_x\) (surface system) at element centroid. Uses the surface-x keyword.

keywords.name: resultant-qy
keywords.description: stress resultant \(Q_y\) (surface system) at element centroid. May use the surface-x keyword.

keywords.name: stress-intermediate
keywords.description: principal intermediate stress \((\sigma_2)\) at element centroid for an element with an elastic material model. Uses the depth-factor keyword, and may also use the surface-x keyword.

keywords.name: stress-maximum
keywords.description: principal maximum stress \((\sigma_1)\) at element centroid for an element with an elastic material model. Uses the depth-factor keyword, and may also use the surface-x keyword.

keywords.name: stress-minimum
keywords.description: principal minimum stress \((\sigma_3)\) at element centroid for an element with an elastic material model. Uses the depth-factor keyword, and may also use the surface-x keyword.

keywords.name: stress-xx
keywords.description: stress ( \(xx\) -component, global system) at element centroid for an element with an elastic material model. Uses the depth-factor keyword, and may also use the surface-x keyword.

keywords.name: stress-xy
keywords.description: stress ( \(xy\) -component, global system) at element centroid for an element with an elastic material model. Uses the depth-factor keyword, and may also use the surface-x keyword.

keywords.name: stress-xz
keywords.description: stress ( \(xz\) -component, global system) at element centroid for an element with an elastic material model. Uses the depth-factor keyword, and may also use the surface-x keyword.

keywords.name: stress-yy
keywords.description: stress ( \(yy\) -component, global system) at element centroid for an element with an elastic material model. Uses the depth-factor keyword, and may also use the surface-x keyword.

keywords.name: stress-yz
keywords.description: stress ( \(yz\) -component, global system) at element centroid for an element with an elastic material model. Uses the depth-factor keyword, and may also use the surface-x keyword.

keywords.name: stress-zz
keywords.description: stress ( \(zz\) -component, global system) at element centroid for an element with an elastic material model. Uses the depth-factor keyword, and may also use the surface-x keyword.

keywords.name: name
keywords.description: assign a name s to the history record (used to reference the history elsewhere)

keywords.name: component-id
keywords.description: the element with component ID ind is selected.

keywords.name: depth-factor
keywords.description: the depth factor at which elastic element stresses are computed (see the structure liner recover stress command). If this keyword is not specified, then the depth factor is set to +1. Only available if noted.

keywords.name: integration-point
keywords.description: the integration point at which stresses are sampled. The integration point locations are shown here . If this keyword is not specified, then the integration point index is set to 1 . Only available if noted.

keywords.name: node
keywords.description: the node location to use when retrieving values from the element. Must be from 1 to 3 . If this keyword is not specified, then the node location is set to 1 . Only available if noted.

keywords.name: position
keywords.description: the element with a centroid closest to v is selected

keywords.name: side
keywords.description: the side of an embedded liner. Must be 1 or 2 , with default value of 1 .

keywords.name: surface-x
keywords.description: the surface-x vector whose projection onto the element plane defines the x-direction of the surface coordinate system (see the structure liner recover surface command). If this keyword is specified, then the surface coordinate system of the three nodes used by the element will be modified.
